title: capacity.v1
description: Duration/token distributions per (task_class x node x model x tool) bucket, projected from the HEARTH ledger. Feeds the CP-SAT job-shop scheduler (JS2).

evidence_watermark.description: ISO timestamp of the newest event seen while building this document, or null if the ledger was empty.

bucket_count.description: len(buckets); the corpus regression guard's primary count field for capacity.json (CQRS/ES plan step 2).

corpus_digest.description: Content-shaped fingerprint of the ledger this document was projected from: sha256 over 'events.ndjson:<non-blank line count>' (CQRS-ES-STANDARDIZATION.md step 4). Optional/additive.

corpus_event_count.description: Non-blank ledger lines consumed while building this document. Optional/additive.

ok_rate.description: ok/determinate for this bucket, or null when the bucket has no determinate evidence at all. Null means 'never learned', NOT 'always failed'.

indeterminate.description: Calls excluded from ok_rate because they reported ok:false while naming no error -- a structurally incoherent shape that ledger.new_event now refuses to persist, retained here so historical corpora stay auditable rather than silently reweighted. Optional/additive.

calls_7d.description: Calls in the trailing 7-day window, counted like lifetime `calls` (indeterminate included). Window is the half-open interval (evidence_watermark - 7d, evidence_watermark] -- anchored to the corpus, never to wall-clock now, so re-projecting an unchanged ledger is stable. Optional/additive.

ok_rate_7d.description: ok/determinate within the trailing 7-day window, or null when that window holds no determinate call. Null means 'no recent evidence', NOT 'always failed' -- consumers should fall back to lifetime ok_rate. Prefer this over ok_rate when asking 'is this healthy NOW': a resolved outage ages out here but depresses the lifetime rate forever. Optional/additive.

calls_30d.description: Calls in the trailing 30-day window; see calls_7d. Optional/additive.

ok_rate_30d.description: ok/determinate within the trailing 30-day window; see ok_rate_7d. The steadier of the two windows -- prefer it when 7d holds too few calls to be worth reading. Optional/additive.
